meaning: n.[生]端粒(在染色体端位上的着丝点)
examples: Now a telomere is a highly repetitious and genetically meaningless sequence of DNA, what we were calling junk DNA.
Every time the Chromosome divides—every time one cell divides into two—pieces of the ends of the Chromosome, the telomere, get broken off.
Anyway, if we examine these ends of these coils of DNA, we will find a sequence of DNA at each end of every human Chromosome, called a telomere.
She also said telomere research has a higher proportion of women than other fields because in its early days, the lead researchers brought women into the field.
"We now need to know more about how early life conditions can influence the pattern of telomere loss and the relative importance of inherited and environmental factors, " she said.
